phrases.french: Une table en terrasse, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.english: A table on the terrace, please.
phrases.phonetic: ewn TAH-bluh on teh-RAS, seel voo PLAY
phrases.context: The pedestrianised Place Drouet d'Erlon is Reims's main social square — lined with cafés, brasseries, and restaurants. Lively day and night.

phrases.english: What are your Champagne region specialities?
phrases.context: Look for: potée champenoise (hearty stew), andouillette (pork sausage), biscuits roses de Reims (pink biscuits traditionally dipped in champagne), and local cheeses like Langres.

phrases.french: Vous avez des biscuits roses de Reims ?
phrases.english: Do you have the pink biscuits of Reims?
phrases.phonetic: voo-zah-VAY day bees-KWEE ROHZ duh RANS
phrases.context: Biscuits roses de Reims are the city's most famous edible souvenir — light pink finger biscuits made since the 18th century, traditionally dipped in champagne or rosé.

tips: Biscuits roses de Reims are the edible souvenir of the city — available in most shops and cafés
The place is at its best on warm evenings when all the terraces fill up
Try the local Langres cheese — a washed-rind cheese from nearby Haute-Marne, sometimes served with champagne poured into the dimple on top
